public_functions.behavior_description: Enable and clock the eGTM module. Configure the eGTM CMU to provide the ATOM submodule clock from the selected FXCLK source and set the DTM/CDTM clock source as required. Initialize the unified PWM configuration with defaults, select the ATOM submodule and assign three contiguous channels (base + 2 sync) for a 3-phase inverter. Set center-aligned mode with a 20 kHz frequency on the base channel and enable synchronized start/update across all configured channels. For each of the three channels, assign an output configuration that drives complementary outputs via the dead-time module (CDTM) with active-high polarity for the main output and active-low for the complementary output; configure the pads in push-pull mode with appropriate pad drive strength. Configure deadtime for both rising and falling edges to 1 microsecond using the DTM/CDTM during initialization, then issue a single immediate deadtime update after init to latch and synchronize the values across all channels; reserve further immediate deadtime updates only for cases where deadtime must be changed at runtime. Set up a single period-event interrupt on CPU0 with the requested priority, attach it to the first (base) channel, and leave other channels without interrupts. Initialize the PWM driver with the prepared config and per-channel configurations, update global control/unit data if required by the driver, install the ISR handler and initialize interrupt routing, then start all channels synchronously so they run in lockstep.
public_functions.doxygen:
/*!
 * @brief Initialize and start the eGTM ATOM 3-phase inverter PWM.
 *
 * @param void No parameters.
 * @return void
 *
 * @note Side effects:
 *       - Enables and configures eGTM/CMU/DTM clocks.
 *       - Initializes ATOM PWM channels and configures output pins.
 *       - Configures deadtime and synchronized channel start.
 *       - Installs the period-event ISR on CPU0 and enables its interrupt.
 *       - Modifies global GTM state and starts PWM outputs.
 */

public_functions.behavior_description: Maintain an array of three duty cycle values, one per phase. On each call: for each phase, check whether adding a fixed STEP value would reach or exceed 100%. If so, wrap that phase's duty to 0.0 before applying the increment. Then increment all three phase duties by STEP. After updating all three values, apply them immediately to the PWM driver using the multi-channel immediate update API so that the new duty cycles take effect coherently across all configured channels while preserving center alignment and configured deadtime.
public_functions.doxygen:
/*!
 * @brief Update duty cycles for all three phases and apply them immediately.
 *
 * @param void No parameters.
 * @return void
 *
 * @note Side effects:
 *       - Updates internal duty array and triggers immediate multi-channel duty update.
 *       - New duties take effect coherently across configured channels.
 */

private_functions.doxygen:
/*!
 * @brief Period-event interrupt service routine for the eGTM PWM.
 *
 * @param void No parameters.
 * @return void
 *
 * @note Side effects:
 *       - Executes in interrupt context; keep processing minimal.
 *       - May acknowledge/clear the period-event source.
 */

/*!
 * @brief Callback invoked on each PWM period event.
 *
 * @param void No parameters.
 * @return void
 *
 * @note Side effects:
 *       - Runs in interrupt context when called by the ISR.
 *       - Should perform quick, non-blocking work only.
 */
